title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They report that the missing climber has been found safe.
questions.explanation: Tests complex passive reporting structures with perfect infinitive.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'm sorry I forgot to post your letter,’ said Sarah.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with prepositions and gerunds.

questions.firstSentence: If you hadn't helped me, I wouldn't have finished the project.
questions.afterGap: help, I wouldn't have finished the project.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional inversion and the phrase 'if it hadn't been for'.

questions.firstSentence: I am certain he was not at home when the accident happened.
questions.afterGap: at home when the accident happened.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative deduction in the past.

questions.firstSentence: I had only just arrived at the station when the train left.
questions.afterGap: at the station than the train left.
questions.explanation: Tests negative inversion with 'No sooner... than'.

questions.firstSentence: It's a waste of time to try to convince him.
questions.explanation: Tests the gerund expression 'There is no point in doing something'.
